description:
You are a cloud-migration architect who just received a legacy SQLite
    database that must be migrated to a new location and table name.  
    Everything you need is already on-disk.

    1.  Legacy material  
        • Source database: /home/user/legacy/legacy.db  
        • Contains exactly one table: customers_old  
        • Schema of customers_old  
              id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
              name         TEXT  
              email        TEXT  
        • The table already contains three rows of data.

    2.  Target requirements  
        • Create a brand-new database file: /home/user/new/new.db  
        • Inside new.db create a table called customers with
              id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
              full_name    TEXT  
              email        TEXT  
        • Migrate every row from customers_old
          so that “name” is copied into “full_name”.  
        • Every value coming from the source must be
          bit-for-bit identical in the destination (except for the column name
          change).

    3.  Verification log  
        • After the migration, write /home/user/migration_report.txt.  
        • The file must have exactly the six lines shown below, in
          precisely this order, no extra whitespace, no blank trailing
          lines:  

              MIGRATION_STATUS: SUCCESS
              SOURCE_DB: /home/user/legacy/legacy.db
              SOURCE_TABLE: customers_old
              DEST_DB: /home/user/new/new.db
              DEST_TABLE: customers
              ROWS_TRANSFERRED: 3

    4.  Constraints  
        • Treat this as a “single-command” task: once you are ready,
          perform the entire migration with one shell command of your
          choice (it may contain pipes, redirects, or a single call to
          sqlite3 that executes multiple SQL statements).  
        • Use only standard Linux utilities available in a minimal
          distribution (sqlite3 is installed).  
        • Do NOT hard-code absolute paths inside SQL statements—use shell
          redirection or sqlite3’s filename arguments instead.  
        • No sudo privileges are available; everything must live under
          /home/user.

    5.  Success criteria checked by automated tests  
        a. /home/user/new/new.db exists.  
        b. new.db has a table named customers with 3 rows.  
        c. Selecting all columns from customers returns the same id and
           email values found in customers_old, and full_name matches the
           original name column exactly.  
        d. /home/user/migration_report.txt exists and
           its contents match the six-line block shown in step 3 verbatim.

    Execute the migration now.
truth:
Pre-created items before the agent starts:

    Directories
    ├── /home/user/legacy
    └── /home/user/new          (exists but is empty)

    File: /home/user/legacy/legacy.db
      • SQLite database containing table customers_old
      • SQL used to create and populate it:
            CREATE TABLE customers_old (
                id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                name  TEXT,
                email TEXT
            );
            INSERT INTO customers_old (id, name, email) VALUES
               (1,'Alice Smith','alice@example.com'),
               (2,'Bob Jones','bob@example.com'),
               (3,'Charlie Brown','charlie@example.com');

    Verification expectations after task completion:

    1. /home/user/new/new.db exists and is a valid SQLite DB.  
    2. Inside new.db:
         .tables       → lists customers (only)  
         PRAGMA table_info(customers);  → shows
                0|id|INTEGER|0||1
                1|full_name|TEXT|0||0
                2|email|TEXT|0||0
         SELECT * FROM customers ORDER BY id;
                1|Alice Smith|alice@example.com
                2|Bob Jones|bob@example.com
                3|Charlie Brown|charlie@example.com

    3. /home/user/migration_report.txt contents (exact, no trailing spaces):
            MIGRATION_STATUS: SUCCESS
            SOURCE_DB: /home/user/legacy/legacy.db
            SOURCE_TABLE: customers_old
            DEST_DB: /home/user/new/new.db
            DEST_TABLE: customers
            ROWS_TRANSFERRED: 3
